title: Sticherus monticola
taxonomy: Sticherus monticola is a species of fern in the family Gleicheniaceae. This plant is classified as a vascular plant, specifically a type of pteridophyte. It belongs to the order Gleicheniales and the family Gleicheniaceae, which is a small family of ferns. The genus Sticherus is a part of the Gleicheniaceae family and contains several species of ferns. The classification of Sticherus monticola reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Sticherus monticola is known to be used in traditional medicine, particularly in New Caledonia. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Some studies suggest that it may have potential in treating various health conditions, including pain and fever. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications and effectiveness. Traditional healers in New Caledonia continue to use the plant to treat various ailments, often in combination with other local remedies.
